auditor: Chartered Professional Accountants, Licenced Public Accountants
one_time_description: Per Capita Distributions from $88.4 million flood claim settlement paid to Naotkamegwanning Community Trust; $25.4 million utilized for legal fees and per capita distributions during the year
economic_notes: Impact Benefit Agreement with New Gold Inc. (Rainy River Project) providing minimum $125,000/yr metal production payments, minimum $125,000/yr royalty payments, and $50,000/yr education payment. Paagitoon Limited Partnership (janitorial services, 99.9% owned) did not operate in 2023. Mining revenue of $1,644,310 received.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to non-compliance with PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations — management did not complete assessment of tangible capital assets for potential AROs. Major one-time item: $88.4 million flood claim settlement paid to community trust; $25.4 million flowed through statements as per capita distributions. ISC flood claim loan proceeds of $1,752,836 also recognized in Other income upon settlement. Consolidated entities include: Naotkamegwanning Education Authority, Naotkamegwanning Health and Social Services, Shawendaasowin Child and Family Services. GBE: Paagitoon Limited Partnership (janitorial services, 99.9% owned, did not operate in 2023). No schedule of remuneration included in financial statements. Band Support Programs expenses of $28M appear inflated due to allocation of per capita distribution and flood settlement related costs. Housing units inferred from CMHC mortgage notes (9+12+10+10+7+8+4 = 60 units).
